book_claims.claim: Eating 2+ servings of vegetables per day associated with longest lifespan among Loma Linda Adventists
book_claims.mechanism: Rich in fiber, vitamins, minerals, antioxidants, and phytonutrients; supports cellular defense and repair

book_claims.claim: Vegetables are a core component of all centenarian blue-zone diets and associated with reduced cancer and cardiovascular disease
book_claims.mechanism: Anti-inflammatory phytonutrients, fiber, and micronutrients; supports detoxification pathways

book_claims.claim: A variety of vegetables provides essential fatty acids, vitamins, and minerals that nourish the immune system — a major defense against cancer; specific vegetables for cancer prevention include green peppers
book_claims.mechanism: Dense micronutrient content supports immune function, cellular protection, and anti-inflammatory pathways

book_claims.claim: Plant-based diets rapidly alter gut microbiota from an inflammatory Western-diet profile to a less inflammatory one
book_claims.mechanism: Plant-based foods promote beneficial gut bacteria that regulate immune cell activity and reduce systemic inflammation

supplementary_research.finding: Bell peppers are among the richest dietary sources of vitamin C (PMID 32516501); a single red pepper supplies over 150% of daily vitamin C needs, supporting antioxidant defense and collagen synthesis
Carotenoids including beta-carotene and capsanthin in red bell peppers have demonstrated anti-inflammatory and anti-carcinogenic properties in cell and animal studies (PMID 26561072)

Bell peppers score among the highest of common vegetables for antioxidant capacity; red peppers have significantly more vitamin C, carotenoids, and flavonoids than green, supporting longevity-relevant oxidative stress reduction

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Water-soluble; best preserved raw or lightly cooked; co-ingestion with iron-rich foods enhances non-heme iron absorption
Fat-soluble; absorption significantly increased with co-ingested fat such as olive oil
Unique xanthophyll with anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activity; fat-soluble
Supports neurotransmitter synthesis and immune function; bioavailable from fresh peppers

synergies.description: Fat from olive oil dramatically enhances absorption of fat-soluble carotenoids (beta-carotene, capsanthin) from bell peppers
High vitamin C content boosts non-heme iron absorption from legumes such as lentils, fava beans, and peas
Both are lycopene/carotenoid-rich Mediterranean vegetables that combine well for anti-inflammatory, anti-cancer phytonutrient coverage
